title: Unsupervised Detection Path
description: Build fluency in Unsupervised Detection inside Detection Techniques by moving from foundational terms into adjacent methods, variants, and extensions.

steps.title: k-Means
steps.summary: k-Means belongs to the Detection Techniques area of AI/ML, within Unsupervised Detection. It sits inside Detection Techniques / Unsupervised Detection. Compare it with Outlier Detection and Anomaly Detection. It opens into K-means++ and Fuzzy C-Means.
steps.whyIncluded: Start here to anchor the vocabulary and core intuition for this area.

steps.title: Outlier Detection
steps.summary: Outlier Detection belongs to the Detection Techniques area of AI/ML, within Unsupervised Detection. It sits inside Detection Techniques / Unsupervised Detection. Compare it with Anomaly Detection and Coverage-based Anomaly Detection. It opens into Outlier Detection Techniques and Boosting for Outlier Detection.
steps.whyIncluded: Start here to anchor the vocabulary and core intuition for this area.

steps.title: Anomaly Detection
steps.summary: Anomaly Detection belongs to the Detection Techniques area of AI/ML, within Unsupervised Detection. It sits inside Detection Techniques / Unsupervised Detection. Compare it with Coverage-based Anomaly Detection and Clustering-based Anomaly Detection. It opens into Coverage-based Anomaly Detection and Clustering-based Anomaly Detection.
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: Clustering-based Anomaly Detection
steps.summary: Clustering-based Anomaly Detection belongs to the Detection Techniques area of AI/ML, within Unsupervised Detection. It sits inside Detection Techniques / Unsupervised Detection. Start after Anomaly Detection and Model-Based Clustering.
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: Coverage-based Anomaly Detection
steps.summary: Coverage-based Anomaly Detection belongs to the Detection Techniques area of AI/ML, within Unsupervised Detection. It sits inside Detection Techniques / Unsupervised Detection. Start after Anomaly Detection and Model-based methods.
steps.whyIncluded: Treat this as an advanced extension once the earlier terms feel natural.
